diagnosis.score_headline: A Brand That Only Exists on Amazon
diagnosis.score_summary: The 0/100 alignment score represents a total brand void. There is no inconsistency between two competing strategies; there is a fully-realized brand on the PDP and a complete absence of brand signaling on the WWW. This isn't brand schizophrenia, it's brand abandonment, ceding all narrative control to the marketplace.

truth_1.headline: An Identity By Default
truth_1.gap_description: The WWW communicates no positioning, forcing customers to guess what the brand stands for. The PDP has been forced to invent a position, defining the brand as 'The Gentle Giant'—a trusted, mass-market daily essential. Your marketplace is creating your strategy for you.
truth_1.evidence: [POSITIONING] WWW: No Position Defined vs. PDP: 'The Gentle Giant' — a 'Daily Essential' with a 'Balanced' aesthetic
truth_1.why_matters: When a brand refuses to define itself, the market defines it as a commodity. By having no POV on your own website, you signal that you are nothing more than a product on a shelf, entirely dependent on the algorithm for survival.
truth_1.the_hard_choice: Your PDP has successfully positioned you as a trusted 'Gentle Giant,' a strategy that is clearly working. The choice is stark: either formally adopt this identity and build it out on your WWW to create a cohesive brand (Option A), or continue to let your brand exist only as a marketplace entity, forever vulnerable to the next private label competitor (Option B). A brand without a home is just a tenant, waiting to be evicted.

truth_2.headline: Silent Treatment vs. Problem Solver
truth_2.gap_description: Your WWW says nothing, offering no reason to believe. Your PDP has a clear, compelling message: it is 'The Problem Solver' that can 'banish bacteria' and 'keep skin soft.' One channel is a library, the other is a sales floor.
truth_2.evidence: [MESSAGING] WWW: No Message vs. PDP: 'The Problem Solver' — 'banish bacteria, keep skin soft...protects skin from dryness'
truth_2.why_matters: A brand that doesn't articulate its own value proposition has no value proposition. You are forcing customers who seek information on your site to leave empty-handed, breaking the chain of trust and making the purchase feel purely transactional.
truth_2.the_hard_choice: The marketplace has proven that a direct, benefit-driven 'Problem Solver' message converts. You must decide: either embrace this messaging on your own website to build brand equity around your solution (Option A), or remain silent and accept that your brand story will only ever be told by your retail partners (Option B). If you don't tell people why you exist, they'll assume you have no reason to.

truth_3.headline: No Proof vs. Clinical Proof
truth_3.gap_description: The WWW offers zero proof points, appearing unconfident and illegitimate. The PDP, in contrast, anchors its entire argument in 'Derm-Validation,' relentlessly leveraging the '#1 Dermatologist Recommended' claim. You are an expert on Amazon and an amateur on your own turf.
truth_3.evidence: [KEY SELLING POINTS] WWW: No Validation vs. PDP: 'Derm-Validation' — 'DERMATOLOGIST #1 RECOMMENDED'
truth_3.why_matters: Trust is not transferable; it must be earned on every channel. By failing to present your most powerful proof point on your own website, you make the claim feel like a borrowed tactic rather than a core truth, undermining its power.
truth_3.the_hard_choice: Your most valuable asset is the trust dermatologists place in you. The hard choice is whether to treat this as a core brand pillar or a disposable sales tactic. Option A: Rebuild your WWW experience around the authority of 'Derm-Validation.' Option B: Continue to withhold your best evidence from your own site, implicitly telling customers your real home is Amazon. Trust that is not proudly owned is quickly lost.

truth_4.headline: An Identity on Loan
truth_4.gap_description: This is a hidden risk, not a bright spot. The only place a customer can form an identity with your brand is on the PDP, where you signal 'The Pragmatist.' This identity is entirely dependent on Amazon's ecosystem, making it a single point of failure.
truth_4.evidence: [SELF-EXPRESSION] WWW: No Identity Signal vs. PDP: 'The Pragmatist' — making 'The Smart Choice'
truth_4.why_matters: A brand identity that only exists on a third-party platform is not an asset; it's a rental. If a competitor out-positions you on pragmatism or Amazon's algorithm changes, your brand's entire meaning system vanishes overnight because it has no foundation.
truth_4.the_hard_choice: Your marketplace presence has created a fragile but valuable identity for your customers. The choice is existential: either invest in building a brand-owned home for 'The Pragmatist' on your WWW to make the identity permanent (Option A), or do nothing and accept that your brand's soul is subject to the terms and conditions of another company (Option B). You cannot build a kingdom on rented land.

truth_5.headline: The Brand Home is Mute
truth_5.gap_description: This is an untapped strength. Your PDP has discovered a powerful, modern voice—'The Hype Beast'—using witty, energetic copy like 'scared skingdom.' This proves a legacy brand can speak a modern language, yet this voice is completely absent from your silent WWW.
truth_5.evidence: [TONE OF VOICE] WWW: Silent vs. PDP: 'The Hype Beast' — 'Banish bacteria from your scared skingdom...your senses are Euro trippin’'
truth_5.why_matters: Your most engaging and personality-driven asset is quarantined on the marketplace. You are leaving brand affinity, engagement, and a massive opportunity to modernize your perception on the table by keeping your own website a sterile, silent void.
truth_5.the_hard_choice: Your Amazon team is out-innovating your brand team. They've proven a 'Hype Beast' voice works. You must decide: either adopt this successful voice globally and bring your brand into the modern era (Option A), or muzzle your most creative channel and force it to conform to a non-existent brand standard (Option B). Your marketplace is showing you the future; you have to choose to see it.

marker_1.so_what: Without a stated purpose on its own channel, the brand's 'Guardian' role on PDP feels tactical, not authentic, risking commodity status.

marker_2.so_what: The lack of a WWW mission means the brand has no declared ambition, making the PDP's 'Democratizer' play feel opportunistic rather than intentional.

marker_3.so_what: By not stating its values on its own site, the brand's 'Safety First' claims on PDP lack the foundational authority they need to be fully believed.

marker_4.so_what: The PDP has carved out a clear 'Gentle Giant' position, but without WWW reinforcement, it remains a tactical play, not a core brand strategy.

marker_5.so_what: A vibrant 'Luxury Icon' personality exists on the PDP, but its value is completely lost without a brand-owned home to anchor it.

marker_6.so_what: The PDP's sophisticated archetype mix creates a compelling character, but it's a character in a play with no home theater, limiting its impact.

marker_7.so_what: The PDP has developed a winning, modern 'Hype Beast' voice that is completely untapped by the core brand, representing a massive missed opportunity.

marker_8.so_what: A strong, trust-building 'Pharma-Code' visual system on the PDP is undermined by the lack of a consistent visual home on the WWW.

marker_9.so_what: Clear 'Problem Solver' messaging on the PDP drives conversion, but the absence of this on the WWW prevents the brand from owning its solution.

marker_10.so_what: The PDP tells a compelling 'Lab Breakthrough' story, but it feels hollow without a brand-owned platform to tell that story with depth and authority.

marker_11.so_what: The powerful 'Derm-Validation' proof point on the PDP lacks ultimate authority because it's not reinforced on the brand's own website.

marker_12.so_what: The brand's unique 'Tech Engineer' USP exists only on the PDP, preventing it from becoming a true, ownable brand asset.

marker_13.so_what: The PDP's 'Science Education' focus builds trust effectively, but this trust is isolated to the transaction point, not the brand relationship.

marker_14.so_what: A clear functional benefit of 'Restoration' is communicated on the PDP, but the brand fails to build equity around this promise on its own channel.

marker_15.so_what: The powerful emotional benefit of 'Relief / Safety' is proven on the PDP but is not being leveraged to build a deeper brand connection on the WWW.

marker_16.so_what: The brand offers a 'Pragmatist' identity to customers only on Amazon, making the identity contingent on the marketplace, not the brand itself.

marker_17.so_what: The social proof of being 'The Safe Choice' is a powerful sales driver on PDP, but it's not being translated into long-term brand equity on the WWW.
